source_pr.title: plan: support aggregation push down.
source_pr.body:
Like select sum(a.id) from t a, t b where a.id = b.id can be optimized as
select sum(agg) from (select sum(a.id) as agg, id from t group by id) a, t b where a.id = b.id.

@shenli @coocood @zimulala @XuHuaiyu @tiancaiamao PTAL

If you want more details, please refer to https://www.overleaf.com/read/rzgxhpbbfgjq
I am keeping to complete it.

symptoms: result = tk.MustQuery("select count(*), a.c from t a , t b, t c where a.c = b.d and b.d = c.d group by c.d order by a.c") (executor/aggregate_test.go)
failure_signature: result = tk.MustQuery("select count(*), a.c from t a , t b, t c where a.c = b.d and b.d = c.d group by c.d order by a.c") (executor/aggregate_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: result = tk.MustQuery("select count(*), a.c from t a , t b, t c where a.c = b.d and b.d = c.d group by c.d order by a.c") (executor/aggregate_test.go)
